data.title: Acadian Timber appoints Malcolm Cockwell as interim CEO
data.description:
• Acadian Timber (ADN:CA) on Wednesday announced Malcolm Cockwell has been appointed interim President & Chief Executive Officer, effective immediately.
  • Cockwell succeeds Adam Sheparski, who has stepped down as President & CEO and as a Director of the company to pursue other opportunities.
  • Cockwell has served as Chair of the company since August 2019. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Molson Coors Non-GAAP EPS of $1.21 beats by $0.06, revenue of $2.66B misses by $50M
data.description:
• Molson Coors press release (TAP): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.21 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $2.66B (-2.9% Y/Y) misses by $50M.

  • 2026 OUTLOOK

    We expect to achieve the following targets for full year 2026 despite the inherent uncertainties that exist with inflationary commodity cost pressures and a softer beer industry.

    • Net sales: flat, plus or minus 1% versus 2025 on a constant currency basis.
    • Underlying income (loss) before income taxes: decline in the range of 15% to 18% versus 2025 on a constant currency basis.
    • Underlying earnings per share: decline in the range of 11% to 15% versus 2025.
    • Capital expenditures: $650 million incurred, plus or minus 5%.
    • Underlying free cash flow: $1.1 billion, plus or minus 10%.
    • Underlying depreciation and amortization: $720 million, plus or minus 5%.
    • Consolidated net interest expense: $260 million, plus or minus 5%.
    • Underlying effective tax rate: in the range of 22% to 24% for 2026.
  • Shares -7.81% AH.

data.title: Blue Owl Capital Corp. Q4 earnings meet consensus; TII trails
data.description:
Blue Owl Capital Corp. (OBDC) delivered Q4 earnings on Wednesday that met the Wall Street consensus as its adjusted total investment income fell short of expectations.

The business development company's stock, though, surged 5.7% in after-hours trading as OBDC and two sister BDCs announced agreements to sell $1.4B of assets, giving OBDC greater flexibility to deploy capital.

Q4 adjusted net investment income per share of $0.36, matching the average analyst estimate, compared with $0.36 in Q3 and $0.47 in Q4 2024.

Q4 adjusted total investment income of $439.5M, vs. the $450.9M consensus, declined from $446.3M in the prior quarter and increased from $394.4M in the year-ago period.

Net asset value per share of $14.81 at Dec. 31, 2025, dipped from $14.89 at Sept. 30, driven primarily by credit-related markdowns on a small number of names, partly offset by accretive share repurchases.

Also on Wednesday, Blue Owl's (OBDC) approved a new repurchase program of up to $300M of common stock, replacing its prior $200M authorization approved in November 2025.

"OBDC closed the year with strong fourth-quarter earnings and credit performance, reflecting the health of our borrowers and our defensive, senior secured strategy focused on the upper middle market," said CEO Craig W. Packer. "Demonstrating our conviction in OBDC's strategy and long-term value, we repurchased approximately $148M of OBDC's common stock during the quarter, accretive to NAV per share and the largest quarterly repurchase activity in our history."

Total investments at fair value fell to $16.5B at Dec. 31, 2025, from $17.1B at Sept. 30.

Net investment commitments during the quarter totaled $684M, and sales and repayments were $1.3B. That compared with $1.3B in new investment commitments and $797M of sales and repayments in Q3. 

Conference call on Feb. 19 at 10:00 AM ET.

data.title: Insulet outlines 21%–23% Omnipod revenue growth for 2026 with operating margin expansion, driven by global adoption and product innovation
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Insulet Corporation (PODD) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Ashley McEvoy, CEO, reported "we closed 2025 with another strong quarter, recording our 10th consecutive year of 20% or greater constant currency revenue growth," citing durable recurring revenue and a profitable business model as key drivers. She highlighted total company revenues of $784 million for the quarter, 29% growth in constant currency, and surpassing $2.7 billion in full year revenue.
  • McEvoy emphasized the company's leadership in automated insulin delivery (AID), particularly with Omnipod 5, and noted record new customer starts across both U.S. and international markets, with the majority transitioning from multiple daily injections. She stated, "Omnipod's strong clinical evidence, broad access, affordability and ease of use are enabling us to expand well beyond traditional endocrinology channels."
  • CEO McEvoy discussed momentum in the U.S. type 2 diabetes market, with type 2 new customer starts growing significantly both sequentially and year-over-year, and the prescriber base expanding 62% to over 6,500 clinicians in 2025. She described international performance as "standout," supported by launches in Canada, Australia, and the Middle East, along with positive price/mix realization from the transition to Omnipod 5.
  • McEvoy announced that in 2026, Insulet will introduce new algorithm updates, expand CGM integrations, and launch Omnipod Discover, a data platform to enhance prescriber and patient experience. She added, "In 2026, we will continue to purposely increase R&D investment to advance our next-generation platforms, including Omnipod 6 as well as our fully closed-loop system for type 2 diabetes."
  • Flavia Pease, CFO, stated, "delivering over $780 million in total revenue, an increase of 31.2% at reported rates and 29% at constant currency rates." She highlighted record new customer starts, stable retention rates, and expansion of gross margin to 72.5% for the quarter and 71.6% for the year. Pease also noted a 50% increase in R&D spending in Q4, and 37% for the full year.

Outlook

  • Pease outlined 2026 guidance, stating, "we expect our total Omnipod revenue to grow 21% to 23% and our total company revenue to grow 20% to 22%."
  • U.S. Omnipod revenue is expected to grow 20% to 22% in 2026, with international Omnipod revenue projected to grow 24% to 26%. Pease attributed the deceleration in growth to difficult comparisons due to the anniversary of the U.S. type 2 launch and annualizing recent international rollouts.
  • The company expects approximately 100 basis points of operating margin expansion for the full year, reflecting top line growth, modest gross margin improvement, and a step-up in R&D investments.
  • Pease also shared, "we expect 2026 adjusted EPS to increase by more than 25%."

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q4 2025 was reported as $784 million, with U.S. revenues of $568 million and international revenues of $214 million.
  • Full year 2025 revenue surpassed $2.7 billion, with $1.9 billion from the U.S. and $754 million from international markets.
  • Pease reported a fourth quarter gross margin of 72.5% and full year gross margin of 71.6%. Adjusted operating margin was 18.7% for Q4 and 17.6% for the year. Adjusted EPS was $1.55 for Q4 and $4.97 for the year.
  • Free cash flow for 2025 exceeded $375 million, including a $70 million tax benefit. Capital expenditures for Q4 were $135 million, mainly attributed to manufacturing expansion in Malaysia and Costa Rica.

Q&A

  • Jeffrey Johnson, Baird: What is the most underappreciated part of the Insulet story? McEvoy: "I would highlight 4 key areas. Number one is our tech lead...number two is our growing commercial prowess...three is our manufacturing at scale; and four is our financial strength."
  • Robert Marcus, JPMorgan: How do you sustain new patient growth in the U.S. and internationally? McEvoy responded that balanced growth is driven primarily by new entrants from MDI, with type 2 at nascent stages and a strong clinical value proposition.
  • David Roman, Goldman Sachs: Can you help reconcile script trends to reported revenue? Pease said, "the best...would be to use total Pods as that's the best reflection of the future revenue outlook," noting script data has limitations.
  • Larry Biegelsen, Wells Fargo: How sustainable is 20% growth amid new competition? McEvoy emphasized Insulet's head start, barriers to entry, and ongoing investment in clinical performance and commercial efforts. Pease added, "We have best-in-class gross margin...We are free cash flow positive."
  • Michael Polark, Wolfe Research: Is Insulet ready for Dexcom's 15-day G7 sensor? Benjamin stated, "Omnipod 5 is compatible with the 15-day G7 now."
  • Travis Steed, BofA: How has guidance philosophy changed? Pease: "We continue to set guidance with a full intent to deliver. That has not changed."
  • Joanne Wuensch, Citi: What should we look for at ADA? McEvoy and Benjamin highlighted upcoming data on the fully closed-loop system for type 2 and pivotal results from the STRIVE study for Omnipod 6.
  • Richard Newitter, Truist: Does 40% of new starts from type 2 signal type 1 growth leveling off? Pease said type 1 grew year-over-year and was comparable to Q3; type 2 growth is accelerating due to lower penetration.
  • Danielle Antalffy, UBS: How does the in-practice sampling program work? McEvoy described a capital-efficient approach with strong conversion ratios and expanding prescriber base.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised questions on sustainability of growth, competition, and new patient acquisition, indicating a neutral-to-slightly-positive tone with focus on long-term differentiation and execution.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, emphasizing execution, innovation, and competitive moats. McEvoy repeatedly pointed to the company's "track record," "tech lead," and "financial strength."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management continue to focus on market expansion and competition, though management's confidence appeared more pronounced in this call, especially regarding barriers to entry and innovation pipeline.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from raising 2025 forecasts last quarter to explaining decelerating growth rates for 2026 due to tough comps and annualizing major launches.
  • Strategic focus remains on expanding type 2 penetration and international markets, with new emphasis on next-generation platforms (Omnipod 6, closed-loop system).
  • Analysts this quarter focused more on sustainability, competitive threats, and growth drivers, while management placed greater emphasis on technology leadership, scale, and financial performance.
  • Key metric changes include a step-up in R&D spending, continued margin expansion, and a projected increase in EPS for 2026.
  • Management's tone shifted from strong optimism to highlighting durable moats and explaining growth normalization amid high comps.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited challenging year-over-year comparisons as a factor in decelerating growth guidance for 2026.
  • Analysts questioned sustainability of growth, competitive threats from new entrants, and the balance of type 1 versus type 2 growth.
  • Pease pointed to ongoing investments in innovation, commercial scale, and manufacturing as mitigation strategies, stating the company remains "well positioned to continue investing robustly for future growth while delivering meaningful margin expansion."

Final Takeaway

Insulet’s management highlighted another year of robust growth and successful global expansion, underpinned by a strong recurring revenue model, expanding market share in both type 1 and type 2 diabetes, and continued innovation through Omnipod 5 and next-generation platforms. For 2026, the company projects 21% to 23% Omnipod revenue growth and increased operating margins, supported by investments in R&D, manufacturing, and commercial capabilities. Management remains focused on sustaining leadership in AID, driving further penetration in underpenetrated markets, and delivering long-term value for shareholders through consistent execution and disciplined investment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tenaris GAAP EPS of $0.87 beats by $0.10, revenue of $3B beats by $40M
data.description:
• Tenaris press release (TS): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.87 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $3B (+5.3% Y/Y) beats by $40M.
  • Shares +1.53%.

  • Market Background and Outlook

    Although oil and gas prices remain volatile amidst contrasting near-term oversupply and geopolitical concerns, oil and gas companies are looking at a resilient longer-term demand outlook and the need to replace production declines as they advance their investment plans. Drilling activity in the United States and Canada is expected to remain near current levels after the modest decline seen in the second half of 2025. In the rest of the world, we do not expect major changes compared to current activity levels in the near term.

    In the United States, despite the increase in tariffs on imported steel products, OCTG prices are still around the same level as before the application of the tariffs. We expect that they will eventually respond to the tariffs on imports and the increases in raw material costs for domestic producers.

    For the first quarter of 2026, we expect our sales and margins to remain close to current levels.

data.title: Nordson Non-GAAP EPS of $2.37 in-line, revenue of $669.46M beats by $16.68M
data.description:
• Nordson press release (NDSN): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.37 in-line.
  • Revenue of $669.46M (+8.8% Y/Y) beats by $16.68M.

  • Outlook

    The Company enters the second quarter with increased backlog, up approximately 4% over the prior year. Order entry momentum was broad-based in the quarter with particular strength in our ATS segment. These trends position the Company to deliver second quarter fiscal 2026 sales in the range of $710 to $740 million. Second quarter adjusted earnings are forecasted to be in the range of $2.70 to $2.90 per diluted share.

    Based on the strong start to the year, the second quarter outlook and the current foreign exchange rate environment, the Company is increasing its full year guidance. Sales are now expected to be in the range of $2,860 to $2,980 million and adjusted earnings to be in the range of $11.00 to $11.60 per diluted share.

data.title: Carvana's Q4 profitability loses traction; shares crater more than 20%
data.description:
Carvana (CVNA) sold more cars than Wall Street expected in the fourth quarter, but concerns over the company’s profitability caused shares to crater in Wednesday’s late trading.

The used-car marketplace experienced a 58% surge in revenue to another record high of $5.60B, beating estimates of $5.26B, with double-digit percentage gains in both retail vehicle sales and wholesale sales.

On a per-unit basis, retail vehicle sales were up 43% to 163,533 and 5K more than anticipated, while wholesale vehicle sales increased 66%. This contributed to a 38% gain in gross profit of $1.05B.

However, breaking down the numbers further shows a $244 decline in the gross profit per vehicle (-$354 on an adjusted basis) and an 18.2% decline in wholesale marketplace gross profit.

The company’s profitability gave a mixed picture, as adjusted EBITDA increased to $511M but missed $539M estimates, while on a per-share basis, adjusted profits surged to $4.22 per share from $0.56 per share, although this was attributed to one-time charges and so is not comparable to $1.12 estimates. Additionally, adjusted EBITDA margin narrowed 100 basis points to 9.1%.

The company did not issue sales guidance for the full year but said it continues to expect to sell 3M cars between 2030 and 2035 and anticipates an adjusted EBITDA margin of 13.5% in FY26.

Carvana (CVNA) shares last traded with a loss of more than 23%.
